ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_consumption" (
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entry in the table */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded energy consumption */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the energy consumption was recorded */,
  "electricity_kWh" REAL /* Total electricity consumption in kilowatt-hours */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of energy (e.g., grid, solar, wind) */,
  "cost_estimate" REAL /* Estimated cost of the energy consumed */,
  "weather_condition_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather condition */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building the data belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_weather_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_condition_id") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("weather_condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the environmental data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded environmental data */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the environmental data was recorded */,
  "rh_percent" REAL /* Relative humidity percentage */,
  "temperature_C" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "dew_point_C" REAL /* Dew point temperature in Celsius */,
  "pressure_mbar" REAL /* Barometric pressure in millibars */,
  "solar_radiation_W_m2" REAL /* Solar radiation in watts per square meter */,
  "wind_direction" REAL /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "wind_speed_m_s" REAL /* Wind speed in meters per second */,
  "cloud_coverage" REAL /* Cloud coverage percentage */,
  "precipitation_mm" REAL /* Precipitation in millimeters */,
  "visibility_km" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "air_quality_index" REAL /* Air quality index */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building the data belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_data_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "buildings" (
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each building */,
  "building_name" TEXT /* Name of the building */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the building */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the building */,
  "building_type" TEXT /* Type of building (e.g., office, retail, residential) */,
  "total_area_sqm" REAL /* Total area of the building in square meters */,
  "number_of_floors" INTEGER /* Number of floors in the building */,
  "construction_year" INTEGER /* Year the building was constructed */,
  "renovation_year" INTEGER /* Year the building was last renovated */,
  "energy_efficiency_rating" TEXT /* Energy efficiency rating (e.g., A, B, C) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupancy_data" (
  "occupancy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupancy record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded occupancy rate */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the occupancy data was recorded */,
  "occupancy_rate" REAL /* Occupancy rate as a percentage */,
  "number_of_people" INTEGER /* Number of people present in the building */,
  "occupancy_zone" TEXT /* Zone or area within the building where the occupancy was measured */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building the data belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupancy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_occupancy_data_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building associated with the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., abnormal consumption) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken in response to the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., open, closed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building the setting applies to */,
  "setting_description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_building_id FOREIGN KEY ("building_id") REFERENCES buildings ("building_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded weather condition */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the weather condition was recorded */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition type (e.g., sunny, rainy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the weather condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_condition_id")
)
